The scenes are associated with the release years 1994-2015, which correspond to the decades {1990-1999}, {2000-2009}, and {2010-2019}. The scenes from 1994 belong to {1990-1999}, scenes from 2004 and 2007 belong to {2000-2009}, and scenes from 2012 and 2015 belong to {2010-2019}. To avoid consecutive scenes from the same decade, we need to alternate between these sets. The optimal sequence that minimizes the total shooting days is to arrange scenes in the order of 4-day, 6-day, 8-day, 10-day, and 12-day scenes, ensuring the decades alternate. One such arrangement is: 1994 (4 days), 2004 (6 days), 2012 (10 days), 2007 (8 days), 2015 (12 days). This sequence alternates between {1990-1999}, {2000-2009}, {2010-2019}, {2000-2009}, {2010-2019} and results in a total of 4+6+10+8+12=40 days. The total shooting days is minimized to 40 days while satisfying the constraint.
